days.title: Life events: the timeline of a life

vocab.exampleEn: She grew up in a village.

grammar.title: Past tense of life-event verbs
grammar.explanation: Russian past tense is built from the infinitive: drop **-ть** and add **-л** (he), **-ла** (she), **-ло** (it), **-ли** (plural/they). It agrees with the **gender and number of the subject**, not the person. So *я родился* (man) but *я родилась* (woman). Reflexive verbs keep **-ся** after a consonant and **-сь** after a vowel: *родил**ся***, *родила**сь***.

examples.english: I was born in 1990. (male speaker)
She grew up in Kiev.
We moved to Moscow.

reading.title: Моя жизнь
reading.text: Я родился в маленьком городе. Там я вырос и учился в школе. Потом мы переехали в Москву.
reading.translation: I was born in a small town. I grew up there and studied at school. Then we moved to Moscow.

days.speakingPrompt: In 3-4 sentences, say where you were born, where you grew up, and where you went to school. Use родился/родилась, вырос/выросла and учился/училась.

days.title: Big moments: work, marriage, family

grammar.title: Past tense with masculine subjects ending in a consonant
grammar.explanation: Many verbs form a clean masculine past with **-л**: *работал*, *начал*, *закончил*. Watch the stress on некоторых verbs: *нача**л*** (he began) but *на**ча**ла* (she began) — the stress can shift to the ending in the feminine. Verbs like **найти** are irregular: past is *нашёл* (m), *нашла* (f), *нашли* (pl).

examples.english: He started a new job.
She found a job at a bank.
We finished school together.

reading.title: Важный год
reading.text: В тот год он закончил университет. Потом он нашёл работу и женился. Свадьба была очень красивая.
reading.translation: That year he finished university. Then he found a job and got married. The wedding was very beautiful.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe an important year in your life or someone you know. Use закончил/закончила, нашёл/нашла and начал/начала with the correct gender ending.

days.title: Narrating a day: morning to evening

vocab.exampleEn: I woke up early in the morning.
She got up at seven o'clock.
In the morning I went to work.
He returned home in the evening.

vocab.english: to lie down, go to bed

grammar.title: Telling a day in order: sequencing past actions
grammar.explanation: To narrate a day, chain past-tense verbs with connectors: **сначала** (first), **потом** (then), **после этого** (after that), **наконец** (finally). Note irregular pasts: **пойти → пошёл / пошла / пошли**; **лечь → лёг / легла / легли**; **встать → встал / встала / встали**.

examples.english: First I woke up, then I got up.
She went to work in the morning.
In the evening we returned home.

reading.title: Мой день
reading.text: Утром я проснулся и встал. Потом я приготовил завтрак и пошёл на работу. Вечером я вернулся домой.
reading.translation: In the morning I woke up and got up. Then I made breakfast and went to work. In the evening I returned home.

days.speakingPrompt: Narrate your day yesterday in 4-5 sentences using сначала, потом and вечером. Include проснулся/проснулась, пошёл/пошла and вернулся/вернулась.

days.title: Genitive review: 'of', possession and absence

vocab.exampleEn: I will tell the story of my life.
This is the beginning of the story.
At the end of the day I was tired.

grammar.title: Genitive review: possession, 'from', and 'have'
grammar.explanation: The **genitive** answers *кого? чего?* (whose? of what?). Typical endings: masculine **-а/-я** (*друг → друга*), feminine **-ы/-и** (*школа → школы*), neuter **-а** (*начало → начала*). Use it for: **possession** (*дом друга* — the friend's house), after **из/без/у** (*из города*, *без друга*, *у меня*), and to express **'have'** with *у меня был/была/было/были*.

examples.english: This is my friend's story.
I came from a big city.
My sister had a car.

reading.title: Начало истории
reading.text: Это история моего друга. Он приехал из маленького города. У него не было работы, но потом он нашёл хорошую работу.
reading.translation: This is my friend's story. He came from a small town. He had no job, but then he found a good job.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell a short story about a friend using the genitive: where they came from (из...), what they had or didn't have (у него был / у неё не было...), and one thing that happened.

days.title: Saying when: time and dates in the past

vocab.exampleEn: At that time I lived in Moscow.

grammar.title: Time expressions with the past tense
grammar.explanation: Time adverbs anchor a story in the past: **вчера** (yesterday), **тогда** (then/at that time), **недавно** (recently), **давно** (long ago). To say 'a week/year ago' use the genitive after **назад** placed *after* the phrase: *неделю назад* (a week ago), *год назад* (a year ago). Note: *неделю* and *год* take the accusative-of-duration form here, but the pattern is fixed — learn it as a chunk.

examples.english: Yesterday an interesting thing happened.
A year ago I moved.

examples.russian: Тогда у нас не было денег.
examples.english: Back then we had no money.

reading.title: Что случилось
reading.text: Недавно случилась интересная история. Год назад мой друг переехал в новый город. Вчера он вернулся, и мы были очень рады.
reading.translation: Recently an interesting thing happened. A year ago my friend moved to a new city. Yesterday he came back, and we were very glad.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell what happened recently and what happened a year ago in your life. Use недавно, год назад, тогда and случилось.

days.title: Putting it together: a full short story

grammar.title: Structuring a narrative: beginning, middle, end
grammar.explanation: A good spoken story uses **opening, sequence, and closing** markers. Open with **однажды** (one day) or **давно** (long ago). Sequence with **сначала... потом... после этого...**. Close with **наконец** (finally) or **в конце** (in the end). Keep all verbs in the **past tense** and watch gender agreement throughout.

examples.english: One day I went to the shop.

examples.russian: Сначала было трудно, потом стало легко.
examples.english: At first it was hard, then it became easy.
Finally I understood this story.

reading.title: Однажды
reading.text: Однажды мой друг рассказал мне историю. Сначала я не помнил её, но потом вспомнил. Наконец я понял, что это была правда.
reading.translation: One day my friend told me a story. At first I didn't remember it, but then I recalled it. Finally I understood that it was true.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell a short story (5-6 sentences) from your life using однажды, сначала, потом and наконец. End with how it finished or what you remember.

days.title: Review: telling your story

grammar.title: Recap: past tense + genitive for storytelling
grammar.explanation: This week you practised the **past tense** (drop **-ть**, add **-л/-ла/-ло/-ли**, agreeing with the subject's gender and number) and reviewed the **genitive** (possession *дом друга*, 'from' *из города*, 'without' *без друга*, and 'have' *у меня был/была*). You also learned to **sequence a story** with однажды, сначала, потом, наконец. Remember irregular pasts: *пошёл/пошла*, *нашёл/нашла*, *лёг/легла*.

examples.russian: Однажды я приехал из другого города.
examples.english: One day I came from another city.

examples.russian: У неё не было работы, но потом она нашла её.
examples.english: She had no job, but then she found one.

examples.russian: Сначала мы встали, потом пошли завтракать.
examples.english: First we got up, then we went to have breakfast.

reading.title: Как прошёл год?
reading.text:
— Как прошёл твой год?
— Сначала я нашёл работу, потом переехал в новый город. А ты?
— У меня родился ребёнок! Это был лучший год.
reading.translation:
— How did your year go?
— First I found a job, then I moved to a new city. And you?
— I had a child! It was the best year.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell the story of your past year in 5-6 sentences. Use the past tense throughout, at least two genitive phrases (из..., у меня был...), and the sequencing words сначала, потом, наконец.
